model_stance_conclusion_text: I lean towards advocating for reform rather than complete abandonment.

nodes.node_text: 1. **Personal Freedom**: Some argue that marriage can be seen as a restrictive institution that limits individual freedom.

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage is confining and restricting and serves no purpose
marriage makes you lose your personal freedom and having to share everything with your partner, it should be abandoned
Marriage restricts freedom. A person can't do as he or she pleases without considering the needs and wishes of his/her spouse.
marriage should be abandoned because a person is able to have way more freedom
we should abandon marriage it restricts the freedom of an individual

nodes.node_text: By abandoning marriage, individuals could have more flexibility in their relationships without the legal and societal expectations that come with being married.

argument_list.matched_argument: by adopting such practice we can get rid of all the burdens associate with marriage like financial, legal and social obligations.
Marriage restricts freedom. A person can't do as he or she pleases without considering the needs and wishes of his/her spouse.
marriage should be abandoned because a person is able to have way more freedom

marriage tends to force people to remain in a relationship because of a piece of legal paper instead of having the freedom and ability to follow their own happiness which is vitally important for all involved.
we should abandon marriage it restricts the freedom of an individual

nodes.node_text: 2. **Equality and Fairness**: Critics of marriage often point out that traditional marriage structures can perpetuate gender inequalities.

argument_list.matched_argument: Marriage as a social institution is based on outdated notions of exchanging property and other resources in order to purchase or take ownership over a woman, and these practices should be abandoned.
marriage has roots in patriarchy and misogyny therefore it is time to move on to a new system.
marriage is a dated institution that unfairly benefits the patriarchy.
marriage is a social construct to oppress women & is backed by & enforced by the patriarchy

marriage is an archaic form of oppression against women and should be abandoned. it is used to tie a woman to a man to support the man in his success while diminishing the woman to a subservient role.
marriage is an institution founded on the outdated notion that women are property and should be discontinued
marriage is an institution that holds women down.
marriage is an out-dated patriarchal system of female ownership that has no place in a modern equal society.
marriage is an out-dated patriarchal system of ownership of women that has no place in modern societies.
marriage puts women in the subservient role and keeps them there.
Marriage reinforces traditional social norms of the woman in the household and the male being the provider, which both entrenches patriarchal and heteronormative ideas within our society.
some say that marriage is inherently misogynistic

nodes.node_text: Abandoning marriage might lead to more equitable relationships where roles and responsibilities are shared equally based on mutual agreement rather than societal norms.

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage just isn't necessary any more as male and female roles have changed tremendously.
Marriage reinforces traditional social norms of the woman in the household and the male being the provider, which both entrenches patriarchal and heteronormative ideas within our society.

nodes.node_text: 3. **Economic Independence**: Marriage can sometimes lead to financial dependency, which can be problematic if the relationship ends.

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage is an outdated concept now that there is equality in the workplace which allows couples to have financial independence

nodes.node_text: Without marriage, individuals might maintain greater economic independence and control over their own resources.

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage is an outdated concept now that there is equality in the workplace which allows couples to have financial independence
marriage should be abandoned because a person is able to have way more freedom
we should abandon marriage it restricts the freedom of an individual

nodes.node_text: 4. **Legal Complexity**: The legal framework surrounding marriage can be complex and costly, especially in cases of divorce.

argument_list.matched_argument: Abandoning marriage means no ugly divorce battle if things don't work out.
by adopting such practice we can get rid of all the burdens associate with marriage like financial, legal and social obligations.
marriage in an obsolete institution that legally entangles people and does not let them free without incuring costs.
Marriage is an outdated concept which cause emotional trauma when it doesn't work out and people have to dissolve it legally.

marriage is an outdated insitution that is not seen in the same way as it was years ago tying two people together and costs both parties a huge amount of money to get out of if they want to end the marriage.
marriage is nothing but a legal contract between two people, the process can be greatly simplified under a different and less contentious guise

marriage was started as a contract to support a wife and children.   in today's world many marriages fail causing a great cost to divorce,  it would be easier and less expensive not to have the contract at all.
since it is an outdated concept. modern unions consist of many different types of couples who are unlikely to be life long partners and marriage creates a costly legal nightmare when they breakup.
we should abandon marriage because it just causes people to have to spend huge amounts of money on attorney fees should they decide they do not want to live together any longer.
when a marriage breaks down it can cause a lot of stress and money to legally end it. it would be simpler if there was no marriage in place to start and both parties could just move on.

nodes.node_text: Simplifying or eliminating these legal structures could reduce stress and financial burden for many people.

argument_list.matched_argument: by adopting such practice we can get rid of all the burdens associate with marriage like financial, legal and social obligations.
marriage, weddings and divorces, they cost thousands and thousands and mean very little, often causing more stress and upheaval than happiness
there is no need for marriage in this society.  too many marriages end in divorce.  it would be easier to just get rid of that altogether.  safe money and stress.
when a marriage breaks down it can cause a lot of stress and money to legally end it. it would be simpler if there was no marriage in place to start and both parties could just move on.

nodes.node_text: 1. **Social Stability and Support**: Marriage provides a stable structure for raising children and can offer emotional and financial support within families.

argument_list.matched_argument: committment and stability are important in the lives of children and marriage provides these qualities
marriage is an important institution that can provide a secure foundation for raising children.
Marriage is associated with stable families; stable families produce happier children.
marriage is the ideal stable relationship to raise children and helps people to live longer more stable lives.
Marriage makes raising children easier as there are two parents who can provide emotional and financial support.
marriage provides a more stable enviroment in which to bring up children
marriage provides a stable and secure home for the children and should not be abandoned.
marriage provides much needed emotional and financial stability for children.
marriage provides stability and it helps families
marriage provides stability for the individual and for the family.   happily married people are more productive in society
marriage stabilizes the family unit and can make relationships that much closer.
to many, marriage is incredibly important and provides security and a family foundation
Tying oneself to a partner in the form of marriage is often necessary to face the heavy burdens of both capitalism and rearing a family.
We should not abandon marriage since it will increase  single parenting and create unstable households for children

nodes.node_text: It also serves as a foundation for community and societal stability.

argument_list.matched_argument: Marriage has been the foundation of the family unit for ages and abandoning it would contribute to the breakup of society.
marriage is one of the essential elements of society. it is centered around the concept of forming strong, coherent and dependable family.
marriage is the bedrock of society that allows for it's continuation and abandonment could cause the collapse of cohesion, normative agreement and morality for civilization.

Married couples have become a foundational part of how we build our societies, raise our children, and pass down history and wealth, and abandoning marriage altogether would have a number of unforeseen impacts on our societies.
we should not abandon marriage because it is an important commitment that helps keep society going.
we shouldn't abandon marriage because is important for society and union for people

nodes.node_text: 2. **Rights and Benefits**: Married couples often enjoy certain legal rights and benefits, such as tax advantages, inheritance rights, and access to health care decisions.

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage can show commitment and values it can give you rights and privileges under social security law and tax benefits
marriage is a legal relationship so you can claim certain benefits and get inheritance when your loved ones dies.
marriage is a straight-forward way to legalise a union and ensure arrangements such as inheritances and benefits are simplified.
marriage provides many benefits, including financial security, companionship, and legal standing.
the legality of marriage helps with health care and government benefits,
There are many financial benefits associated with marriage.

nodes.node_text: These protections would need to be addressed if marriage were abandoned.

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage offers certain protections and privileges that simply dating or cohabitating can not and do not. it's needed it many instances.
marriage provides protection for couples. it gives them rights with regards to property meaning that if the marriage breaks down one person cannot take everything and leave the other destitute.

nodes.node_text: 3. **Cultural and Personal Significance**: For many, marriage holds significant cultural and personal value.

argument_list.matched_argument: Marriage often serves both culturally and religiously important purposes, and abandoning marriage altogether is likely impossible because of the deep ties it has in a persons identity and conception of the world.
marriage still has cultural and social significance, the joining of two people can truly bring happiness
to abandon marriage would be removing rights that people have bought into as a fundamental part of their existence, it harms no one and is incredibly meaningful for many
to many, marriage is incredibly important and provides security and a family foundation

nodes.node_text: It represents a commitment and love between two people, and abandoning it could be seen as undermining these deeply held beliefs and practices.

argument_list.matched_argument: just because some marriages fail does not mean that the concept of marriage should be ended.  marriage shows commitment and support for another which is great for the world.
Marriage is a meaningful way for a committed couple to show to the world and to each other their level of commitment, and to celebrate their relationship.
marriage is an expression of love and commitment, and should be encouraged, not abandoned
marriage is sacred and confirms a couples commitment to one another.
marriage provides stability and a commitment between people which strengthens relationships and to abandon it would be to turn out backs on something good.
marriage shows a commitment that helps to bond a couple together
we should never abandon marriage.  it means making a commitment and sticking to it through good and bad. it's a way to show someone how much they mean to you.
we shouldn't abandon marriage because it is still sacred for many and shows an act of love

nodes.node_text: 4. **Child Welfare**: In many societies, marriage is seen as the best environment for raising children due to the stability and support it can provide.

argument_list.matched_argument: children achieve better outcomes in life when their parents are married
children have better outcomes in life when their parents are married
committment and stability are important in the lives of children and marriage provides these qualities
Even with high rates of divorce, married couples and the children of married parents ultimately have more stable and emotionally healthy lives than their unmarried counterparts.
it provides a stable environment for children
marriage has shown to create a better environment to raise kids.
marriage is an important institution that can provide a secure foundation for raising children.
marriage is an important institution, it is well know that children who live with both birth  parents in a stable marriage succeed better in life, have better heath and better job prospects.
Marriage is associated with stable families; stable families produce happier children.
marriage is the base of the society. children growing up in married couples are more stable and not stressed than the non-married couples one
marriage is the ideal stable relationship to raise children and helps people to live longer more stable lives.
Marriage makes raising children easier as there are two parents who can provide emotional and financial support.
marriage provides a more stable enviroment in which to bring up children
marriage provides a safe, secure foundation for children.
marriage provides a stable and secure home for the children and should not be abandoned.
marriage provides a stable enviorment for kids
marriage provides a stable environment for children
marriage provides much needed emotional and financial stability for children.
statistically parents who are married tend to stay together more than parents who are not married. this suggests that children will find greater stability in a house where the parents are married.
We should not abandon marriage since it will increase  single parenting and create unstable households for children

nodes.node_text: Removing this institution might negatively impact child welfare if alternative supportive structures are not established.

argument_list.matched_argument: if there was no legal marriage process, family stability would no longer be possible and a the word "responsibility" would become instinct.
We should not abandon marriage since it will increase  single parenting and create unstable households for children

nodes.node_text: While there are valid points on both sides, the decision to abandon marriage entirely seems extreme and potentially harmful given its multifaceted role in society.

argument_list.matched_argument: just because some marriages fail does not mean that the concept of marriage should be ended.  marriage shows commitment and support for another which is great for the world.
marriage is a sacred act in many religions.  there's no reason to abandon it completely.  don't want to get married, don't do.  abandonment is not the answer.

Marriage often serves both culturally and religiously important purposes, and abandoning marriage altogether is likely impossible because of the deep ties it has in a persons identity and conception of the world.

Married couples have become a foundational part of how we build our societies, raise our children, and pass down history and wealth, and abandoning marriage altogether would have a number of unforeseen impacts on our societies.
to abandon marriage would be removing rights that people have bought into as a fundamental part of their existence, it harms no one and is incredibly meaningful for many

nodes.node_text: Instead of abandoning marriage, perhaps a more constructive approach would be to reform and adapt the institution to better meet the needs of modern relationships and individuals.

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage is a wholesome, normal act that teaches family values.  we shouldn't be abandoning the concept but rather fear why more marriages are failing these days and fix it.
we shouldn't abandon marriage instead we should change the ideas about  marriage.

nodes.node_text: This could involve addressing issues like inequality, providing more flexible legal frameworks, and ensuring that all forms of committed relationships receive appropriate recognition and support.

argument_list.matched_argument: "marriage" isn't keeping up with the times.  abandon the old thinking and bring something that incorporates all unions - not just those with a man and woman.
Marriage is an outdated institution. All types of relationships and family units (one-parent families, homosexual relationships, etc.) should be embraced and treated equally.
marriage is out dated and should be replaced by an all-encompassing partnership status that everyone can access
